meta.name: J. Zaki
meta.location: Seawoods, Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India (Open to Global Relocation)

taglines.recruiter: Systems Engineer · 3 years · TCS / SBI GITC
taglines.developer: Infrastructure + AI automation + whatever I'm building this month
taglines.curious: Engineer by day, builder of random things by night

contact_taglines.recruiter: Open to roles in infrastructure, platform engineering, and SRE. Based in Mumbai, open to relocation.
contact_taglines.developer: Always interested in interesting problems. Best reached over email.

summaries.platform_sre: Systems Engineer specializing in Identity & Access Management (IDM) and enterprise infrastructure operations within banking environments. Experienced in leading operations teams and mentoring engineers while managing mission-critical platforms for 30,000+ users. Expert in infrastructure modernization, on-premise containerization, and high-availability systems (99.999%), currently expanding into Platform Engineering, SRE, and multi-cloud architecture.
summaries.management: Systems Engineer and technical coach with a strong foundation in managing mission-critical enterprise banking infrastructure for 30,000+ users. Proven leadership in directing operations teams, mentoring junior engineers, and executing Disaster Recovery drills. Combining deep technical expertise in on-premise containerization with strong executive communication skills, actively preparing for advanced management studies and technical product leadership roles.
summaries.ai_automation: Systems Engineer and automation architect specializing in end-to-end LLM orchestration, GenAI workflow design, and data pipeline engineering. Expert in building secure, high-performance automation frameworks using Python, multi-model API integration (Claude/Gemini), and headless distribution systems. Adept at bridging the gap between legacy enterprise infrastructure restrictions and modern, localized AI workflows to drive operational efficiency.

about.recruiter: Systems Engineer at TCS, deployed at State Bank of India, keeping mission-critical banking infrastructure running at 99.999% uptime for 30,000+ users. Three years of hands-on infrastructure, IDM, and DR execution — with a team of five to lead and a track record of cutting RTO by 50%.
about.developer: I run infrastructure for a Tier-1 Indian bank by day and build automation pipelines, PWAs, and embedded devices by night. My stack spans Linux/RHEL server fleets, Python ETL, Flutter mobile, Astro JAMstack, and ESP32 firmware — usually on free-tier infrastructure and always with a bias toward shipping over theorising. Currently deep into LLM orchestration and GenAI workflow design.
about.curious: I'm an engineer based in Navi Mumbai who finds it hard to stop building things. My day job is keeping banking systems alive for tens of thousands of people. Outside that, I've built an offline Islamic supplication app used by a small community of Muslims worldwide, a running app with ghost routes and crowd radar, a pocket e-ink reader from scratch, and a marketing automation system for my family's marine parts business. I also spent a few months coaching engineers on how to talk about their work — which turned out to be more technical than it sounds.

experience.subtitle: Deployed at State Bank of India (SBI GITC) · Mission-critical IDM & infrastructure for 30,000+ SBI branch users
experience.title: Systems Engineer (IDM & DB)
experience.role_tagline: Deployed at State Bank of India (SBI GITC) · Mission-critical IDM & infrastructure for 30,000+ SBI branch users

bullets.text: Lead an operations team supporting enterprise banking infrastructure and mission-critical IDM/database platforms for 30,000+ internal bank branch users.
Directly mentor a team of 5 junior engineers, fostering technical excellence and adherence to operational standards.
Maintain 99.999% system availability for Tier-1 banking applications.
Administer a multi-environment fleet of 150+ Linux (RHEL) and Windows servers, automating patch management, access governance workflows, and incident response.

Drive infrastructure modernisation initiatives, explicitly shifting focus from cloud migration to on-premise containerisation and Docker-based workflows to streamline deployment cycles within banking compliance constraints.
Architected and executed Disaster Recovery drills and runbooks, successfully reducing Recovery Time Objective (RTO) by 50% for Tier-1 applications.
Collaborate across infrastructure, database, application, and security teams to improve operational reliability, reduce service disruption risks, and ensure strict regulatory compliance.

experience.subtitle: Interview readiness coaching for 50+ engineering and management candidates
experience.title: Technical Soft Skills Coach & Founder
experience.role_tagline: Interview readiness coaching for 50+ engineering and management candidates

bullets.text: Delivered technical interview readiness and executive communication coaching to 50+ engineering professionals and management candidates.
Conducted intensive 1:1 mentorship sessions focused on behavioural frameworks, technical storytelling, and high-impact presentation skills.
Designed and iterated a structured coaching curriculum covering STAR-method framing, stakeholder communication, and senior-level interview strategy.
Sourced and onboarded clients independently, managing end-to-end client relationships across the full engagement lifecycle.

experience.subtitle: Technology & marketing lead for a family marine equipment venture
experience.title: Co-Founder (Technology & Marketing Lead) — Pro Bono
experience.role_tagline: Technology & marketing lead for a family marine equipment venture
experience.featured_metric: 200+ B2B prospects per campaign · Zero hosting cost

bullets.text: Lead all technology and digital marketing operations for a family-owned business specialising in second-hand and refurbished industrial automation and marine equipment parts.

Built and maintain the full digital ecosystem: JAMstack storefront (Astro + Cloudflare), WordPress brand site, professional email infrastructure (GoDaddy/cPanel, SPF/DKIM/DMARC), and product analytics via PostHog.
Engineered a Python-based cold email marketing automation agent integrating Brevo, Google Sheets, Claude API, ZeroBounce, and Hunter.io for hyper-targeted B2B lead generation.
Operate pro bono in a founder-equivalent capacity, applying enterprise-grade engineering practices to a zero-budget independent business stack.

projects.name: Zamaan Marine Digital Ecosystem
projects.one_line_summary: Full digital ecosystem for a family marine parts business — storefront, cold email automation, and analytics at zero hosting cost.
projects.why_i_built_it: My family started a business selling refurbished marine and industrial parts, mostly through eBay. The digital side — website, email outreach, analytics — was an afterthought. I stepped in to build it properly, which meant treating a small family operation with the same engineering rigour I'd apply at work. The constraint of zero budget made it more interesting, not less.

bullets.text: Built a comprehensive digital presence for a business selling second-hand industrial automation and marine equipment parts, reducing time-to-market for new listings by ~60% through eBay API automation and daily CI/CD rebuilds.
Developed an Astro-based JAMstack storefront with eBay API integration, hosted on Cloudflare Pages with automated GitHub Actions rebuild pipeline — achieving zero hosting cost at production quality.

Engineered a full-stack cold email marketing automation agent in Python, integrating Brevo, ZeroBounce, and Hunter.io for lead validation and Claude API for personalised outreach copy — targeting 200+ B2B prospects per campaign cycle.
Implemented professional email infrastructure with SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records, achieving inbox delivery rates above 90% across cold outreach campaigns.
Instrumented product analytics via PostHog, enabling funnel visibility from storefront visit to eBay click-through across all traffic sources.

projects.name: Content Automation Pipeline
projects.one_line_summary: End-to-end pipeline that turns YouTube transcripts into publication-ready Medium articles across 12 niches, with an 85% reduction in time-to-draft.
projects.why_i_built_it: I was writing AWS certification articles for Medium and realised the research-to-draft process was almost entirely mechanical. I built a pipeline to handle the mechanical part — scraping transcripts, structuring content, enforcing quality rules — so the human part (judgment, voice, editing) could actually matter. The "Failure First" framework came from noticing that most AI-generated content fails the same way: it starts safe and stays safe. I built rules specifically to break that pattern.

bullets.text: Architected an end-to-end automation pipeline to transform high-intent YouTube transcripts into professional, 1500+ word Medium articles across 12 distinct niches.
Reduced end-to-end content generation time-to-draft by 85%, enabling rapid scaling across specialised verticals without sacrificing editorial tone.
Developed the "Failure First" Humanisation Framework — a strict LLM governance system eliminating generic AI prose, processing 20,000+ lines of unstructured transcript text per execution run.
Engineered a modular orchestration layer (pipeline.py) managing state tracking via JSON, heuristic validation, and automated GitHub synchronisation via GitPython.
Built a cross-platform desktop GUI using Tauri (Rust bridge) and React 19, enabling local filesystem access and Python execution within a high-performance dashboard.
Integrated Telegram Bot and CLI interfaces for remote/interactive generation, optimising workflows for specialised content verticals.

projects.name: Raaz-o-Neyaz — Islamic Supplications PWA
projects.one_line_summary: Offline-first PWA delivering 80MB of multilingual Islamic supplications — extracted, restructured, and rebuilt from scratch from an Android APK.
projects.why_i_built_it: The existing apps for Islamic supplications were clunky, ad-heavy, or required a constant internet connection. For something as personal as duas and ziyarat — prayers you turn to in quiet moments — that friction felt wrong. I wanted something that worked offline, in your language, on any device, with no ads and no backend. I also wanted to understand the data well enough to build proper search and filtering, which meant reverse-engineering the source database myself.

bullets.text: Engineered a Python reverse-engineering pipeline to extract and restructure a 22,000+ row SQLite database from an Android APK, converting legacy relational tables into optimised JSON, CSV, and PDF formats.
Achieved sub-10ms local query times by restructuring legacy tables into indexed data frameworks — a 4× improvement over the original APK's load performance.
Built an offline-first Progressive Web App using Service Workers and lazy caching to deliver 80MB of multilingual Islamic content (Arabic, Urdu, English) with zero server overhead.
Recruited 40+ beta testers via Reddit (r/shias), gathering structured feedback across iOS Safari, Android Chrome, and desktop environments to drive iterative improvements.
Maintained a zero-cost infrastructure footprint using GitHub Pages for hosting, with no backend dependency.

projects.name: run.to — Activity Platform
projects.one_line_summary: A full-featured Flutter running app with GPS tracking, ghost routes, and crowd radar — built across 6 segments using Clean Architecture.
projects.why_i_built_it: I wanted to understand what it actually takes to build a consumer mobile product end to end — not a tutorial project, but something with real architecture decisions, a real backend, and real UX tradeoffs. Running was the use case because I run, and the features I wanted (ghost routes, nearby runners) didn't exist in a form I liked. Clean Architecture and Riverpod were deliberate choices to stress-test my ability to structure a non-trivial codebase.

bullets.text: Developed a Flutter mobile application for distance-based running, featuring GPS tracking, ghost route generation, and crowd radar across 6 complete feature segments.
Implemented Clean Architecture principles with Riverpod full state machine implementation, producing ~8,000 lines of structured, testable Dart code.
Integrated Supabase and PostGIS for geospatial backend management, supporting sub-100ms proximity queries for nearby runner detection.
Built SQLite offline fallback, ensuring full GPS tracking functionality with zero data loss during network interruptions.
Validated core user flows with 10 internal test users across Android devices, confirming GPS accuracy within 5 metres across all tested routes.

projects.name: ESP-Pocket Reader
projects.one_line_summary: A pocket e-ink reading device built on ESP32-S3 — custom firmware, SQLite content storage, and a Python EPUB ingestion pipeline.
projects.why_i_built_it: Reading on a phone destroys your eyes and your battery life. E-readers exist but they're expensive, locked down, and designed around ecosystems I don't want to be in. I wanted a device that did exactly one thing — display text clearly — and cost almost nothing to run. Building the firmware myself meant I could understand every layer of the stack, from EPUB parsing down to the display driver.

bullets.text: Architected a custom pocket-sized e-ink reading device using an ESP32-S3 microcontroller and a 3.7-inch E-Paper display, achieving a functional firmware prototype with stable display rendering.
Developed embedded C++ firmware (ESP-IDF/PlatformIO) integrating SD card storage and SQLite for robust content management — storing and paginating 50+ EPUB-converted documents in testing.
Engineered a Python EPUB-to-SQLite conversion pipeline, reducing per-book ingestion time to under 30 seconds for titles up to 500 pages.
Planned advanced features including FreeType font rendering and deep-sleep power management targeting <10mA average draw.

projects.name: Personal Remote Development Server
projects.one_line_summary: A persistent cloud Linux dev environment on Oracle Free Tier — full-stack workflows from any device, zero cost, 99%+ uptime over 18 months.
projects.why_i_built_it: My personal projects involve a lot of long-running processes — builds, pipelines, SSH tunnels — that don't play well with a laptop that goes to sleep. I also wanted to be able to develop from my phone when I'm away from a desk. Oracle Cloud's free tier is genuinely powerful if you configure it right, and Zellij with persistent sessions solves the context-loss problem that makes remote development frustrating. This setup has become the backbone of every personal project I've shipped.

bullets.text: Designed and configured a fully persistent remote Linux development environment on Oracle Cloud Free Tier — achieving production-equivalent compute at zero recurring cost (equivalent to ~$50/month on paid tiers).
Optimised for smartphone and laptop accessibility via Zellij terminal multiplexing and Termius SSH, enabling full-stack development workflows from any device with a 4G connection.
Maintained 99%+ uptime over 18+ months of continuous use, with automated session persistence eliminating context loss between work sessions.
Reduced local machine dependency to zero for all personal project development, freeing primary workstation for enterprise tooling only.
